slides.title: 2026 Q2 Operating Review
slides.subtitle: Regional Performance Snapshot
slides.speaker_notes: Open with the top-line result and frame the rest of the deck around three decisions.

slides.title: Agenda
slides.subtitle: What this review covers
slides.speaker_notes: Move quickly through the agenda. This deck is designed for a 12-minute update.

slides.title: Executive Summary

items: Revenue increased by 18% quarter over quarter.
Store productivity improved in East and Central regions.
Refund rate fell by 1.2 percentage points after the checkout revision.

blocks.text: The next priority is to standardize the stronger in-store workflow and reduce training variance.

slides.speaker_notes: Emphasize that the strongest gain came from operational consistency rather than one-time demand spikes.

slides.title: What Changed

items: Checkout flow reduced to four steps.
Regional managers received weekly exception reports.
New SKU bundle tested in 38 pilot stores.
Average queue time dropped to 2.8 minutes.
Pilot bundle lifted basket size by 6%.

slides.speaker_notes: Left column is operational change. Right column is the measurable outcome.

slides.title: Headline Metrics
slides.speaker_notes: Call out the margin improvement and refund reduction together to show better unit economics.

slides.title: Regional Breakdown
slides.speaker_notes: North still underperforms on margin and needs focused store coaching.

slides.title: Channel Trend
slides.speaker_notes: Online and group buying are still smaller channels, but they are growing faster than direct stores.

slides.title: Next Quarter Focus

items: Scale the checkout workflow to all stores.
Codify manager exception handling into SOP.
Expand bundle program after margin review.

slides.speaker_notes: Close on three concrete actions, not a generic summary.
